completed_tasks.name: Scope revision: eval in v0.1.0, gateway in v0.2.0

remaining_tasks.name: Update GTM.md with eval positioning and platform engineering framing
Update CLAUDE.md to reflect revised scope (eval in v0.1.0, gateway in v0.2.0)

decisions.decision: Fork AWS Agent Skill Eval (MIT-0) instead of building eval from scratch
decisions.rationale: Battle-tested with 621 tests, adds trigger reliability + cost Pareto analysis

decisions.decision: Eval suite moves to v0.1.0, pub/sub deferred to v0.2.0
decisions.rationale: Proving skills work > distributing them. Eval makes governance credible

decisions.decision: Skills gateway is part of skillctl (v0.2.0)
decisions.rationale: Runtime enforcement completes the governance story but is separate concern from build-time

decisions.decision: A-F eval grades map to certification tiers
decisions.rationale: A/B=verified, C=community, D/F=rejected. Reuse eval scoring as certification mechanism

decisions.decision: LLM-as-judge layered on top of deterministic eval
decisions.rationale: Rubric-based scoring via Anthropic API adds quality assessment beyond static analysis
Registry, approval, gateway, identity, audit, shadow detection, composability

next_action: Run /gsd:discuss-phase 1 to start planning Phase 1 (CLI and Local Governance)
context_notes: Project initialization and scope review complete. Two scope revisions happened: (1) platform engineering analysis added gateway/identity/observability to vision, (2) AWS Agent Skill Eval discovery moved eval into v0.1.0 replacing pub/sub. GTM.md still needs minor update for eval positioning. CLAUDE.md is the original spec and may need updating to reflect revised scope, but it can also serve as the aspirational reference. The user provided competitive analysis in Italian with 4 competitors. Key threat is Chainguard (entered March 17 2026).
